title: Report finds former Canton Sgt. Sean Goode used ‘horrific’ language in texts
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Canton officials released an internal report detailing offensive messages by former Sgt. Sean Goode targeting various protected groups.
The Massachusetts POST Commission suspended Goode's certification, barring him from working in law enforcement statewide.
Goode resigned during an internal affairs probe; Canton previously placed him on paid leave after learning of his messages.

title: Alvin Campbell Jr., serial rapist and AG’s brother, receives life in prison
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Alvin Campbell Jr. sentenced to life for raping five women and attempting another assault between 2017-2019.
Campbell, 45, posed as an Uber driver outside Boston bars, was convicted on 21 of 22 charges in June.
In addition to life, Campbell receives over 100 years in consecutive sentences; related cases in Northampton pending.

title: Officials identify man killed in wrong-way crash in Boston
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Riley Sergi, 28, of Haverhill, was killed in a wrong-way crash on I-93 in Boston Sunday morning.
Antone Carvalho, 81, of Somerset, drove a Cadillac Escalade the wrong way, causing the multi-vehicle accident.
A family of four was hospitalized after the crash; the incident is under investigation by authorities.

title: Germany vs. Paraguay World Cup live coverage: Round of 32 begins at ‘Boston Stadium’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Germany faces Paraguay at Boston Stadium in the World Cup Round of 32, with kickoff at 4:30 p.m.
Germany advanced despite inconsistent group stage results, including a 7-1 win over Curaçao and a 2-1 loss to Ecuador.
Paraguay reached the knockout stage with a 1-0 win over Turkey after losing 4-1 to the USA and a 0-0 draw with Australia.

title: Revolution announce front office shift: Mass. native Chris Tierney named general manager
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Chris Tierney named general manager and chief soccer officer of New England Revolution, moving from assistant sporting director.
Curt Onalfo promoted to senior vice president of soccer operations, focusing on team development and youth programs.
Tierney, a former Revolution player and front office member since 2019, will report to club president Brian Bilello.

title: Man allegedly pepper sprays woman jogging in apparent random assault in Maine coastal city
author: John Waller
bullets: A man allegedly attacked a jogger with pepper spray in Rockland, Maine, on Saturday morning.
The suspect, 37, is charged with criminal threatening, assault, and using disabling chemicals.
Witnesses chased the man away before police arrested him for the random assault.

title: Noah Kahan asks his audience to please not poop at venues (except in designated areas)
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Noah Kahan requests concertgoers to only use designated bathroom areas after an incident at a Philadelphia concert.
Fans at Kahan's concerts reportedly disrupted events by using seats instead of restrooms.
Kahan also addressed fans stealing street signs in Strafford, offering to pay for replacements.

title: Hiker rescued by helicopter after 50-foot fall in Huntington Ravine
author: John Waller
bullets: A 24-year-old man fell 50 feet on Mount Washington's Huntington Ravine Trail and was rescued by helicopter.
New Hampshire Fish and Game coordinated the rescue with the National Guard using helicopter support due to tough terrain.
The man sustained serious but non-life-threatening injuries and was flown to Dartmouth Hitchcock Medical Center.

title: State trooper relieved of duty after allegedly being captured on video knocking out other trooper in bar fight
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A Massachusetts State Police trooper was relieved of duty after allegedly fighting with another trooper in a Quincy bar.
Surveillance video shows one trooper knocking the other unconscious during an off-duty confrontation at ReelHouse Marina Bay.
The incident is under investigation by the State Police Office of Professional Integrity and Accountability, with a hearing pending.

title: What makes a ship a ‘tall ship’?
author: Madeline Bilis
bullets: Tall ships are identified by their traditional rigging and sail training uses, rather than their height.
The term 'tall ship' originated from a 1902 poem but was not used during the Age of Sail.
Sail Boston 2026 will feature numerous tall ships, emphasizing their historical and cultural significance.
